compiled_state.title: Deep tier re-review dispatches opus arch without prerequisite 3 sonnet reviews
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description
During /dso:sprint Phase 5 Step 7 (Autonomous Resolution Loop), re-review after a fix dispatches dso:code-reviewer-deep-arch (opus) directly without first re-running the 3 prerequisite sonnet agents (correctness, verification, hygiene). 

The deep tier protocol requires: 3 parallel sonnet agents → opus arch synthesis. On re-review, only the opus arch is dispatched, meaning it lacks fresh sonnet findings for the revised diff and may produce inconsistent scores.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run /dso:sprint with a batch that triggers deep tier review
2. Deep tier review fails with a critical finding
3. Resolution sub-agent fixes the issue
4. Re-review dispatches only opus arch, not the 3 sonnet agents first

## Expected Behavior
Re-review should re-run the full deep tier pipeline (3 sonnet + opus synthesis) on the revised diff.

## Actual Behavior
Re-review dispatches only the opus arch agent, which synthesizes stale sonnet findings from the initial review pass.

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral (dual-signal: orchestrator behavior during re-review + workflow file in docs/workflows/)
comments.author: Test
